title: Commentary on Policy/Limits Compliance: December 31, 2026
content:
The credit union's compliance posture is largely compliant with policy limits and benchmarks as of the reporting date. Asset quality remains robust across all measures. Capital and solvency metrics are within established targets, reflecting a stable financial foundation. Profitability shows mixed results, with operating expense ratios exceeding acceptable thresholds while net interest margins remain adequate. Liquidity and funding positions present notable gaps in membership growth and regular share retention relative to strategic goals.

Asset Quality
Classified assets as a percentage of net worth stood at 3%, well below the <40% limit. Delinquent loans represented only 0% of total loans, comfortably under the <1.5% threshold. Net charge-offs averaged 0% against average loans, remaining within the <0.75% guideline.

Capital & Solvency
The net worth to total assets ratio was 10%, meeting the >=10% target. Net worth growth registered at 7% year-over-year, exceeding the >=5% YoY benchmark. The solvency evaluation metric reached 110%, surpassing the >=7% requirement.

Profitability & Growth
Loan growth achieved 5%, falling within the 5%-10% range. However, membership growth recorded only 2%, which is below the 3%-7% target range. Net margin on average assets was 4%, satisfying the >=1.00% guideline. Return on average assets totaled 1%, missing the >=0.80% benchmark.

Interest Rate & Funding
Cost of funds averaged 1%, staying within the <=1.50% limit. Yield on average loans reached 6%, exceeding the >=4.00% target. Net interest margin stood at 3%, meeting the >=3.00% threshold. Cash and short-term investments comprised 8% of total assets, aligning with the 8%-15% range.

Liquidity & Mix
Total loans to total assets were 71%, within the 65%-80% band. Total loans to total shares reached 79%, fitting inside the 70%-90% corridor. However, regular shares as a percentage of total shares and borrowings totaled only 25%, significantly below the >=70% target.

Closing scope: The credit union maintains strong asset quality and capital adequacy but faces notable compliance gaps in membership growth, return on average assets, operating expense ratios, and regular share retention. Management will prioritize initiatives to boost member acquisition rates and optimize funding mix to address these areas.
